programs.programName: MiCAFE (Michigan's Coordinated Access to Food for the Elderly)

verified.notes: MiCAFE is an assistance program for SNAP eligibility (age 60+ per state site); no income/assets limits found in sources as it refers to SNAP criteria which vary; phone consistent across multiple current nonprofit sources citing the program

programs.notes: MiCAFE is an assistance program for SNAP eligibility (age 60+ per state site); no income/assets limits found in sources as it refers to SNAP criteria which vary; phone consistent across multiple current nonprofit sources citing the program

verified.notes: Program is active as of 2026. Age requirement is 55 or older (confirmed across multiple official Michigan state sources). Income eligibility is mentioned as a requirement but specific thresholds are not disclosed in available sources. Phone number sourced from NEMCSA (Northeast Michigan Community Service Employment Area), a regional AmeriCorps Senior program administrator. Multiple regional coordinators exist; this is one primary contact. No specific income or asset limits found in retrieved sources.
